emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin Emblem

The best choice for early aggression. Adaptive penetration ignores base armor, effectively multiplying your damage. Movement speed helps you set up kills — being faster than enemies is crucial.

Tip: Basic adaptive attack + Thrill = maximum damage for the first 4 minutes (before enemies buy defensive items).

Fighter Emblem
Provides more sustain but less damage. Why? More adaptive attack without penetration becomes less efficient against armor.

Jungle Emblem
Must-have for all junglers. Faster rotations open key opportunities:

Free turtle secures
Uncontested ganks
Safe split pushing

Check tournament builds — pros never skip this.

Talent Choices:
War Cry:
+Higher raw damage
+Experienced players who mastered spacing and timing

Killing Spree:
+Movement speed + healing = better tempo
+Universal, consistent choice

Killing Spree is safer and more forgiving. But if you professional and learn distance use war cry.

equips.equip_desc:
Retribution Choice

(Blue):
+Movement speed
+Pairs well with War Cry (compensates lack of mobility)

(Red):
+Bonus damage
+Pairs well with Killing Spree (amplifies burst)

Core Items:

War Axe
HP, sustain, damage (including true damage). Lets you engage first without dying. Better than Corrosion Scythe in current meta — too many CC mages and burst marksmen. Dead Freya = zero damage.

Golden Staff
Infinite skill 2 procs = insane healing and DPS. Essential for split pushing and extended fights.

Brute Force Breastplate
HP, movement speed, defense, damage — all in one. Helps you ignore CC chains. Amazing combo with War Axe for survivability.

Damage Items:

Blade of Despair
Pure damage spike. Build when you're ahead and need to delete targets.

Malefic Roar
Outscales other penetration items after 12+ minutes. Melts tanks and sustain offlaners.
Situational: If enemies have no armor stackers, consider Sea Halberd (anti-heal) or Meteor (mag def and heal) instead.

hero.hero_overview: Freya is a powerful fighter who excels in extended duels and frontline engagements. With her skill kit focused on shields, burst damage, and sustained fights, she becomes stronger the longer the battle goes on. Freya can quickly jump into fights using her gap-closing skill, then stack her passive to enhance her basic attacks and gain shields. In the mid to late game, she shines as a durable damage dealer who can initiate fights or peel for the backline.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong Duels: Excellent 1v1 potential thanks to her shields and enhanced basic attacks.
Good Sustain in Fights: Her passive grants shields that help her survive extended trades.
Reliable Engage: She has a dash that lets her close gaps or chase enemies easily.
High Burst + DPS: Combines burst damage with strong continuous attacks.
Snowball Potential: Once Freya gets items, she becomes very hard to stop.
Best played in EXP Lane or as a bruiser  in team fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Item Dependent: Weak in the early game and needs gold to scale.
Can Be Kited: Struggles against heroes with strong mobility or ranged poke.
Vulnerable to Crowd Control: CC can stop her combos and make her easy to burst down.
Short Range: Has to commit fully to fights, making positioning important.
Mana Reliant Early Game: Can run out of mana if skills are spammed too often.

Freya and Diggie combo is a powerful and popular strategy in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, often referred to as a "dark system" or "IQ combo," due to its ability to neutralize enemy crowd control and allow Freya to dominate team fights. The core of the combo relies on the synergy between their Ultimate skills. 
Combo Description
The combo works by:
Freya initiating a fight with her high burst damage and close-combat skills.
Diggie ensuring Freya remains unstoppable during her assault by providing crucial control immunity at the critical moment. 
How to Execute the Combo
Freya's Initiation: Freya uses her Ultimate, Valkyrie State, to gain shield and full Sacred Orb stacks, preparing her for battle. She then dives into the enemy backline or the middle of a team fight using her first skill, Leap of Faith, which deals damage and pulls enemies together, potentially airborne for a brief moment.
Diggie's Counter: The enemy team will immediately attempt to use their own crowd control (stun, suppress, knock-up, etc.) to stop Freya's assault.
Diggie's Ultimate Activation: The Diggie player must have quick reflexes to activate their Ultimate, Time Journey, as soon as the enemy unleashes their CC abilities. This skill removes all debuffs from nearby allied heroes (including Freya) and grants them control immunity and a shield for 3 seconds.
Freya's Rampage: With control immunity active, Freya is free to spam her second skill, Valkyrie Slash, weave in basic attacks, and continue her relentless attack without being interrupted by enemy abilities. 
Why It Works
Crowd Control Immunity: Freya is a powerful but potentially vulnerable hero if locked down by CC. Diggie's Ultimate completely negates this weakness, allowing her to utilize her full damage output.
Aggressive Synergy: The combo encourages an aggressive playstyle. Freya can confidently dive deep into enemy territory knowing she has a safety net against immediate retaliation.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Freya is a hyper-carry who excels in sustained fights by managing her Sacred Orb stacks and leveraging her attack speed buffs and crowd control. Success with Freya depends heavily on playing aggressively and mastering her unique ability-auto attack synergy. 
Laning Precautions
Mind your range: Freya's standard basic attacks have limited range. Be mindful of long-range poke from enemy heroes, as this can make laning difficult.
Manage Orb Stacks: Utilize her passive by using basic attacks in between skills to regenerate Sacred Orbs. This ensures you have stacks ready for your next ability rotation or escape.
Prioritize Wave Clear: In the early game, use your second ability (Spirit Combo / Pulse) to clear minion waves efficiently. This helps prevent the enemy from pushing you under your tower and allows you to farm safely.
Be Wary of Ganks: Freya is vulnerable to jungle ganks before she has her ultimate or appropriate escape items/spells. Maintain good vision and position yourself on the opposite side of the minion wave from your opponent to make ganks more difficult for the enemy.
Avoid Extended Fights Early (Without Ultimate): While Freya thrives in sustained fights, her ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) is a massive power spike that provides a shield, bonus damage, and ranged basic attacks. Without it, be cautious in all-ins against heroes with stronger early game burst. 
Team Fight Strategies
Timing is Everything: Wait for the right moment to engage. If you jump in too early, you risk being locked down by heavy crowd control and burst down instantly.
Initiate with Ultimate: Activating your ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) before or immediately upon engaging provides a large shield, CC immunity, and turns your basic attacks into ranged splash damage, allowing you to survive the initial burst and deal massive area damage.

hero.hero_overview:
Passive (Power of Einherjar): Basic attacks generate Sacred Orbs (which now also regenerate automatically out of combat). Orbs grant attack speed and are consumed to empower skills.
Skill 1 (Leap of Faith): Freya leaps to a target, dealing damage and pulling enemies toward the center while gaining a shield.
Skill 2 (Spirit Combo/Valkyrie Slash): The core of her kit. She dashes forward to deal damage and slow enemies. In 2025, this skill has no fixed cooldown but consumes Sacred Orbs for each cast, allowing for continuous spamming if orbs are managed well.
Ultimate (Valkyrie Descent): Freya transforms into a Valkyrie, gaining a massive shield, increased attack range, and enhanced basic attacks that splash
hero.hero_strength:
Early Game: Freya is exceptionally strong at level 1-2. Use Skill 2 aggressively to zone opponents and secure early kills. Focus on "zoning" the enemy EXP laner away from minion experience.
Mid Game: Once you have core items like Corrosion Scythe or Berserker's Fury, participate in turtle fights. Use her high burst to target back line mages and marksmen.
Late Game: While powerful, Freya is vulnerable to heavy Crowd Control (CC).
Tactics: Wait for the enemy to expend their CC skills before initiating with your Ultimate.
Combo: Ultimate Skill 1 (to close gap/CC) Skill 2 & Basic Attack spam. Use Skill 1 to chase if the enemy attempts to blink away.
hero.hero_weakness: crowd control (CC), especially stuns and knock-ups that interrupt her combos, her reliance on stacking her passive for damage, and her limited range

dominants.dominant_desc:
Here's a comprehensive guide to playing Freya in Mobile Legends:

*Best Role:* Fighter/EXP Lane

*Abilities:*

- *Passive - Power of Einherjar:* Freya gains attack speed when using skills and builds Sacred Orb stacks through basic attacks, enhancing her skills.
- *Leap of Faith (1st Skill):* Freya jumps to a target location, dealing physical damage and pulling enemies towards her.
- *Spirit Combo (2nd Skill):* Freya attacks in a target direction, dealing physical damage, slowing enemies, and gaining a shield.
- *Valkyrie Descent (Ultimate):* Freya dives down, dealing physical damage and applying a slow to enemies, then enters a Valkyrie state, granting her bonus damage and healing.

*Best Builds:*

- *Sustained DPS:* Warrior Boots, Endless Battle, Blade of Despair, Haas' Claws, Immortality
- *Burst:* Warrior Boots, Endless Battle, Berserker's Fury, Blade of Despair, Immortality
- *Durable:* Warrior Boots, Thunder Belt, Cursed Helmet, Immortality, Brute Force Breastplate

*Recommended Emblems:* Assassin or Custom Assassin Emblem with Fatal, Weapons Master, and Lethal Ignition for increased damage output.

*Battle Spells:*

- *Inspire:* Increases attack speed and adds penetration, synergizing with Freya's passive and crit build.
- *Flicker:* Provides additional mobility for surprise engages or escaping sticky situations.

*Tips and Combos:*

- Use Leap of Faith to initiate fights or chase down enemies.
- Combine Spirit Combo with basic attacks to maximize damage output.
- Activate Valkyrie Descent to deal massive damage and sustain in team fights.
- Prioritize upgrading Spirit Combo and Leap of Faith, then max out Valkyrie Descent.

*Counters:*

- *Freya is weak against:* Franco, Chou, Esmeralda, Kagura, Moskov
- *Freya is strong against:* Odette, Irithel, Alpha, Sun, Zilong

*Playstyle:*

- Play aggressively in the early game to establish dominance.
- Focus on farming and gaining an advantage in the EXP lane.
- Use Freya's mobility and damage to roam and apply pressu

hero.hero_overview:
Freya, the Valkyrie, is a Fighter hero in Mobile Legends Bangbang (MLBB) who specializes in Charge/Burst damage. She is #22 hero in MLBB which was initially released in November 2016. The revamped Freya was recently released on November 5, 2025. Her revamp has given her the ability to constantly use her dash skill and ultimate to deal massive AoE (Area of Effect) damage, making her a formidable initiator and damage dealer in team fights. She relies heavily on her Sacred Orb passive stacks to chain her

skills.

[FREYA'S SKILLS ]

[Passive Skill ] Power of Einherjar: Her Basic Attacks grant Sacred Orbs (max 6 stacks) and also grant a temporary Attack Speed boost after casting any skill, encouraging Basic Attack weaving. Sacred Orbs fuel her Skill 2 (Spirit Combo), allowing consecutive dashes without cooldown.

[SKILL 1] - Leap of Faith: Freya leaps to a target area, dealing damage and briefly pulling enemies towards the center, which is excellent for initiation and isolating a target.

[SKILL 2] - Valkyrie Slash: Freya dashes forward, dealing damage, and slowing enemies. This skill has no cooldown but consumes 2 Sacred Orbs per cast.

[ULTIMATE ] - Valkyrie Descent: Freya dives down to the target which slows the enemies in her path and enters the Valkyrie state. Her basic attacks deal splash damage with bonus damage and recover HP for 12 seconds.

[SKILL LEVEL PRIORITY SUGGESTION ]:

1. Ma

2. A

3.G

b

M

Lvl 5-15: Prioritize ULT > S2 > S1[SKILL COMBOS ]:

Freya's main strength is her ability to constantly use her Skill 2, so the key is always to manage your Sacred Orb stacks by Basic attacks

1. Ganking/Chase: S2 > Basic Atk (1-2x) > S2 > Basic Atk >S2 2. Initiation/ Burst: S1 > S2 > S2 > ULT > S2> Ranged basic atks

3. Instant Teamfights: ULT > S1 > S2 (3x) > S2 > Ranged basic atks

If she is locked down mid-combo, she dies easily.

hero.hero_overview:
A fearless warrior reborn with divine fury—Freya now wields her orbs and shield to dominate duels, leap into chaos, and emerge victorious with relentless combos and lifesteal.

Freya is a fighter who excels in sustained duels and burst trades. Her revamp gives her orb-based mechanics, shield sustain, and gap-closing combos, making her deadly in both EXP lane and jungle.


[ HERO SKILLS ]
- Passive : Power of Einherjar
Basic attacks grant orbs (max 6). Orbs empower her skills and basic attacks.
- Skill 1 : Leap of Faith
Freya dashes to a target, dealing AoE damage and slowing enemies.
-Skill 2: Valkyrie Splash
A 3-hit combo that deals damage and grants a shield. Each hit consumes 1 orb.
-Ultimate: Valkyrie Descent
Freya enters Valkyrie mode, gaining bonus attack speed, lifesteal, and enhanced basic attacks.


[SKILL COMBOS]
Basic Combo (1v1):
Skill 1 >> Basic Attack >> Skill 2 (3 hits) >> Ult >>Continue Basic Attacks
Burst Combo (Teamfight):
Ult >> Skill 1 (initiate) >> Skill 2 (3 hits) >> Basic Attacks >> Reposition
hero.hero_strength:
-Strong 1v1 Duels: Her shield and lifesteal make her hard to kill in close combat.
-Burst + Sustain: Can dive in, deal heavy damage, and survive with her shield and lifesteal.
-Flexible Roles: Viable in both EXP lane and jungle.
Orb Mechanics: Empower her skills for stronger trades and combos.
- Great Initiation: Skill 1 lets her leap into fights or chase down enemies.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Orb Dependent: Needs orbs to maximize damage and shield—weak when uncharged.
- Kiteable: Struggles against ranged poke or CC-heavy heroes.
- Skill Timing Matters: Misusing Skill 2 or Ult can waste her power window.
- Item Reliant: Needs core items to become a threat in mid to late game.

data.title: How to Master the Revamped Freya

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Her Ultimate provides a massive shield and movement speed boost, turning Freya into an almost unkillable dive machine with amplified damage.
His Ultimate (Fatal Links) gathers the entire enemy team. This sets up Freya perfectly to land her Ultimate's AoE splash damage and her S2 knock-up on everyone.

counters.counter_desc: Esmeralda's passive allows her to ignore Freya's shields and steal them, turning Freya's main defensive tool into Esmeralda's sustain.
His Ultimate (Divine Judgment) uses suppression to instantly lock Freya down. This prevents Freya from activating her Ultimate shield or chaining her S2 dashes.

dominants.dominant_desc: Prioritize farming the purple buff and quickly clearing your jungle to hit Level 4. Use Retribution to secure the first Turtle; your Ultimate grants a huge shield and damage boost, making you a strong objective taker early on. Look for a gank attempt only after hitting Level 4 for maximum burst.
dominants.dominant_title: Early Game (Level 1-4) : Fast Clear & Ult Spike

dominants.dominant_desc: Initiate Ganks with Purpose: Freya excels at diving the backline. Look for overextended enemies and use your S1 (Leap) or S2 dashes to secure a kill. The Killing Spree emblem will heal and speed you up for the next rotation. Dominate Objectives: Secure all subsequent Turtles and start looking to melt turrets after successful ganks.

High-Risk Dive: Use your Ultimate and S2 combo to target the enemy Marksman/Mage. Wait for your Tank to initiate or for the enemy team's main CC to be on cooldown before diving. Split-Push Threat: Use your high Attack Speed and damage to aggressively split-push turrets if the enemy is distracted, forcing them to send multiple heroes back.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin Emblem maximizes damage, Physical Attack, and provides excellent late-game scaling.

Thrill (Damage), Weapon Master (Item Attack Boost), Killing Spree (Heal and Speed after a kill for snowballing).

equips.equip_desc:
Tough Boots (Reduces CC duration, adds Magic Defense)

Corrosion Scythe (Adds Attack Speed and a crucial Basic Attack slow effect)

War Axe (Gives high stacking Physical Attack and Movement Speed)

Endless Battle (Provides Lifesteal and huge True Damage burst after every skill)

Malefic Roar (High Physical Penetration to shred armor late game)

Situational Item (e.g., Immortality for a second life, Sea Halberd for Anti-Heal)

hero.hero_overview: Freya is an aggressive Fighter hero specializing in Charge/Burst damage, often played in the EXP Lane. Her unique passive uses Sacred Orbs to fuel near-endless dashes and mobility. She transforms with her Ultimate to become a high-damage, ranged attacker with massive shields for diving.
hero.hero_strength:
👍 Hero Strengths
High Burst and AoE Damage: She deals massive damage and can hit multiple enemies, especially with her ranged Ultimate.

Excellent Mobility and Chase: Her Sacred Orbs allow her to dash repeatedly, making her great at sticking to targets and repositioning.

Strong Sustain and Survivability: Her Ultimate and Skill 2 grant large shields, allowing her to survive aggressive dives.

Objective Control: Her high Attack Speed makes her very fast at taking turrets and the Lord/Turtle.
hero.hero_weakness:

data.title: Ultimate Guide for Revamped Freya

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Atlas can initiate with his Fatal Links (Ultimate), grouping all enemies together. This is the perfect setup for Freya to dive in with her Ultimate and deal massive splash damage to the entire clumped team.

Mathilda's Guiding Wind (Skill 2) provides Freya with an instant dash to either escape a bad dive or get an extra speed boost right before Freya uses her own Ultimate. Her shields also enhance Freya's durability.
Angela's Heartguard (Ultimate) gives Freya a massive shield and extra damage. This almost guarantees Freya survives the initial enemy burst damage after diving and empowers her splash Basic Attacks.

counters.counter_desc: His Ultimate, King's Calling, creates a field that prevents Freya from using her dashes/blinks to exit. This traps her and stops her entire Skill 2 combo.
Obsidia's Ultimate can stop Freya from using her 2nd skill and Ultimate.

dominants.dominant_desc:
🪽 Mind the Orbs: Do not engage aggressively without at least 4-6 Sacred Orbs ready. You need them to chain Skill 2 dashes for offense or retreat.

🪽 Avoid Poke Damage: Freya is still a melee hero; do not stand close to the enemy EXP laner (especially ranged ones) waiting for minions. Clear and Rotate quickly.

🪽 Skill 2 for Clear: Use your Skill 2 to clear minion waves fast, utilizing the dash to reposition and the Passive's Attack Speed boost to secure the farm.

🪽 Save Skill 1 CC: Try to save the enhanced Skill 1 for enemy ganks or to secure the final kill, rather than using it just to trade early damage.

🪽Early Turtle Focus: Freya has high early damage and sustain. When playing EXP lane, prioritize rotating to the first Turtle objective to fight and get the gold lead.

🪽 Be a Late Initiator (Usually): Do not be the first one to walk into a 5v5 unless you are using your Ultimate. Wait for your Tank/Roamer to engage or for the enemy team to use crucial Crowd Control (CC) skills.

🪽 The Ultimate Dive: Use Valkyrie Descent (Ultimate) to jump over walls or obstacles directly into the enemy backline (Mage/Marksman). This is your primary initiation tool.

🪽 Maximize AoE Damage: While the Ultimate is active, your Basic Attacks deal splash damage. Position yourself to hit as many enemies as possible, not just one.

🪽 Watch the CC: Freya is still highly vulnerable to chain CC. If the enemy team has multiple stuns (e.g., Kaja, Chou, Atlas), wait for them to miss or use those skills before diving in.

emblems.emblem_desc:
✨ Assasin Emblem is best for Freya to deal more damage

🪽Rupture: Provides early Penetration, allowing her damage to bypass enemy armor immediately so she can win early duels and snowball effectively

🪽 Weapon Master: This is the core. It massively amplifies the Physical Attack gained from her items (like Blade of Despair), giving her huge damage scaling, especially to her Ultimate's splash attacks.

🪽 Weakness Finder: Adds crucial utility by giving her Basic Attacks a chance to slow the enemy. This helps her counter her biggest weakness (being kited) and lockdown targets for guaranteed eliminations.

✅ Seasoned Hunter: (Jungler) To deal extra damage to Creeps and for faster farming.

equips.equip_desc:
✨ TOUGH BOOTS: The CC reduction helps Freya survive her initiation jump.

✨GOLDEN STAFF: Highly recommended. This item guaranteeing faster Orbs, and Heal (each basic attack in Ultimate form) and higher damage output from her Ultimate.

✨ BLADE OF DESPAIR (BoD): Must-have item. It provides the highest Physical Attack in the game, which is massively amplified by your Weapon Master talent.

✨ ENDLESS BATTLE: Provides Physical Attack, Heal for sustain, and the crucial True Damage effect after using a skill (which Freya does constantly).

✨ MALEFIC ROAR: Provides high Physical Penetration, allowing you to bypass a huge amount of enemy armor, especially late-game tanks.

✨ IMMORTALITY: For extra survivability.

hero.hero_overview:
👑 HERO OVERVIEW AND BACKGROUND 

🪽 Freya is the powerful Fighter known as the Valkyrie of the Northern Vale, whose new skill set focuses on using a resource called Sacred Orb to chain attacks.
🪽 She swore a sacred oath to protect the land, and her combat prowess has been renewed, giving her a major boost in mobility and sustained damage.
🪽 She is now an agile, high attack speed hero who can initiate fights from a distance with her Ultimate and deal massive ranged, area of effect damage.

👑 HERO'S ROLE IN THE GAME

🪽 Primary Role: Fighter. She is a top-tier duelist and skirmisher.
🪽 Lane Role: Best played as the EXP Lane hero or as a Jungler.
🪽 Specialty: High Sustained Damage (DPS), excellent Chase Potential, and powerful team fight initiation.
🪽 Playstyle: Aggressive and Continuous. She is designed to stay in the fight, constantly generating and spending Orbs to keep her skills (especially S2) active.
🪽 Team Fight: Her Ultimate lets her fly in over walls to land on enemies, immediately turning her basic attacks into ranged, splash attacks for area damage.

👑 HERO'S FARMING ROUTE

🪽 EXP Lane (Sidelane): Use her Passive and Skill 2 to clear the minion wave quickly. She is always ready to rotate or fight when the Ultimate is ready.
🪽 Jungler (Core Role): Her Skill 2 has no cooldown but requires Orbs, making her jungle clear extremely fast.

👑 HERO SKILLS

🪽 Passive: (Power of Einherjar) Freya gains Sacred Orbs from Basic Attacks. Consuming Sacred Orbs increases her Attack Speed.
🪽 Skill 1: (Leap of Faith) Freya leaps to the target area, dealing Physical Damage, gaining a shield, and knocking enemies Airborne.
🪽 Skill 2: (Valkyrie Slash)Freya slashes forward, dealing damage and slowing enemies. This skill has no base cooldown. 
🪽 Skill 3: (Valkyrie Descent) Freya dives down to the target area and enters the Valkyrie state. Her Basic Attacks deal splash damage with bonus damage and recover HP for 12s.
hero.hero_strength:
🌾 LANING OR FARMING 

🪽 Fast Clear: Quickly clears minions and camps using her Passive's Attack Speed boost and her dash-based Skill 2.
🪽 High Sustain: Orbs regenerate out of combat, ensuring she is ready to fight or farm without needing to recall often.
🪽 Excellent Chase: Her repeated dash (Skill 2) makes it hard for enemies to escape her in the lane.

💥 TEAMFIGHT

🪽 Distance Initiation: Her Ultimate allows her to fly over terrain to initiate deep into the enemy backline.
🪽 Area Damage: Once the Ultimate is active, her Basic Attacks become ranged and deal splash damage to multiple enemies.

💎 GAME STAGE

🪽 Early: Strong duellist who can easily gain a gold and level lead (snowball).
🪽 Mid: High threat due to fast rotations and strong burst damage once core items are complete.
🪽 Late: Maintains high damage and survivability due to her ranged Ultimate and heal in each basic attack.

✅ FAVORABLE HERO CHARACTERISTICS 

🪽 High Mobility: Can rapidly close gaps and reposition using her Sacred Orb-fueled Skill 2.
🪽 Aggressive Playstyle: Designed to be a durable initiator and continuous attacker.
🪽 Scales Well: Benefits highly from items that increase Attack Speed and Physical Attack.
hero.hero_weakness:
🌾 LANING OR FARMING 

🪽 High Cost of Mistakes: If she uses her dashes (Skill 2) aggressively but fails to secure the kill or Orb regeneration, she is left with few escape tools.
🪽 Vulnerable to Poke: Freya is a melee hero who can be easily harassed by ranged EXP Laners, which forces her to use her scarce Orbs defensively or lose health.

💥 TEAMFIGHT

🪽 Ultimate Dependency: Her power spike and ability to deal AoE damage are completely tied to her Ultimate; without it, her threat level in a major team fight is low.
🪽 Punishable Initiation: Her Ultimate jump is strong but can be easily read and countered by enemy stuns or suppress skills, often leading to her instant death upon landing.
🪽 Single Target Focus: Her damage outside of her Ultimate state is largely focused on one target, making it harder to pressure multiple squishy heroes in a disorganized brawl.

💎 GAME STAGE

🪽 Early: Requiring more focus on basic attacks and orb management to trade effectively.
🪽 Mid/Late: She struggles significantly against team compositions with high, chained Crowd Control (CC), as CC prevents her from generating Orbs and casting her vital Skill 2.

❌ UNFAVORABLE HERO CHARACTERISTICS 

🪽 Orb Reliance: If she is constantly denied the chance to use Basic Attacks (due to being kited or stunned), her mobility and damage output are severely crippled.
🪽 Susceptible to Kiting: Her primary damage source is melee/short-ranged (outside of Ultimate), meaning high-mobility heroes can easily keep their distance and wear her down.
🪽 Build Constraints: The current kit often forces her to prioritize Attack Speed items to generate Orbs, which can compromise her survivability if she ignores defense items.

🪽 RETRIBUTION: As a Jungler, you need Retribution to secure the Turtle and Lord objectives and clear your jungle quickly. Using the Ice Retribution version on enemy heroes also steals Movement Speed, making it easier to chase down targets for your kill combo.

hero.hero_overview:
Freya is a high-impact fighter built around explosive initiation and relentless dueling. Her 2025 revamp enhances her Divine Strike stacks, enabling smoother combos, thicker shields, and more consistent DPS. Freya excels as a tempo-controlling laner who forces aggressive trades, zones enemies from farm, and converts early advantages into unstoppable mid-late game pressure.

In Gold Lane, she dominates close-range marksmen with superior burst and shield cycling. As an EXP laner, she thrives in sustained duels and fast wave clear. When played as a Jungler, Freya becomes an early skirmish powerhouse with strong Turtle control and rapid jungle-clear tempo.
hero.hero_strength:
Unmatched Duel Pressure: Divine Strike stacks grant high sustain, shields, and DPS, allowing Freya to overwhelm opponents in 1v1 and 1v2 exchanges.

Reliable Engage & Chase: Double-dash mobility ensures she can start or finish fights with precision.

Versatile Trade Patterns: Capable of short bursts or extended fights depending on matchup.

Exceptional Snowballing: Early kill leads translate into unstoppable mid-game dominance.

Fast Objective Control: Empowered basic attacks let her melt Turtle, Lord, turrets, and jungle camps quickly.
hero.hero_weakness:

Dependent on Stack Timing: Poorly timed trades reduce burst and survivability.

Kite Weakness: Ranged heroes with disengage tools can delay her entry.

Cooldown Reliance: Missing Leap leaves her vulnerable to counterburst.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Provides massive, unmissable, hard Crowd Control (CC) with his Fatal Links, which groups the enemies tightly. This creates a perfect opportunity for Freya to use her Ultimate and deal massive ranged AoE splash damage to all targets caught in the CC.

Similar to Atlas, Tigreal's Ultimate, Implosion, is a high-impact CC tool that gathers and stuns multiple enemies. This setup is crucial for maximizing the effectiveness of Freya's Valkyrie Descent, allowing her to easily wipe the enemy formation.

Her Ultimate, Heartguard, gives Freya a large shield, extra movement speed, and bonus damage when used on her during a dive. This makes Freya nearly unstoppable, mitigating her weakness to burst damage and allowing her to chase down targets more effectively while in her Ultimate form.

counters.counter_desc: Is a hard counter as his Ultimate prevents Freya from using any dash skills (S2), completely negating her key source of mobility and escape.
Is another strong counter because his Ultimate, Divine Judgment, uses suppression, which stops Freya's dive and ultimate activation entirely and cannot be cleansed.
Actively benefits from Freya's kit, as her constant dashes trigger his Passive, allowing him to endlessly spam his skills and deal massive damage back to her.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Pre-Charge Orbs: Before initiating a fight, always use minions or jungle monsters to charge up to 6 Sacred Orbs. This ensures you have maximum resources for mobility and enhanced Basic Attacks right when the fight starts.

The S2 Dance: Use Skill 2 (Spirit Combo) not just for movement, but as a repositioning tool between Basic Attacks. This helps you constantly adjust your distance, dodge enemy skill shots, and maintain the pressure.

Ultimate Positioning: When using your Ultimate (Valkyrie Descent), aim to land behind the enemy front line. The ranged AoE splash damage is devastating to grouped-up squishy heroes, and positioning behind them cuts off their escape route.

Early Game (Farming & Dueling): Focus on rushing to Level 4. Use your Skill 2 to quickly clear the minion wave. If matched against a weaker hero in the EXP Lane, challenge them with your charged Sacred Orbs; use Skill 1 to initiate and your enhanced Basic Attacks to dominate the duel and secure an early kill. Prioritize reaching your core item, War Axe, as fast as possible.

Mid Game (Ganking & Skirmishes): Rotate aggressively after clearing your lane to gank the Mid and Gold lanes. Your high mobility with Skill 2 makes you an effective surprise threat. Use your Ultimate strategically to guarantee kills during 2v2 or 3v3 skirmishes, ensuring you secure objectives like turrets or the Turtle. Look for prolonged engagements where you can continuously stack War Axe and utilize your high sustain.

Late Game (Backline Dive): Do not initiate solo. Wait for your Tank or Support to apply initial Crowd Control. Your role is to immediately use your Ultimate to leap past the enemy frontline and target the enemy Marksman and Mage. The ranged AoE splash damage is crucial here. Use Skill 2 spam to stick to your targets and continuously evade incoming skill shots, guaranteeing a quick wipe of the enemy backline before retreating.

emblems.emblem_desc:
The Assassin Emblem is ideal for maximizing Freya's burst and damage output, making her a devastating backline disruptor, particularly effective when she is played as the team's main physical damage threat or in the Jungle role.


Tier 1
Thrill (Physical Attack)	

Increases her Physical Attack stat, giving her higher early-game damage on her Basic Attacks and skills, which aids in farming and early dueling.

This is the crucial damage-focused choice, directly increasing the power of her enhanced Basic Attacks and Skill 2 spam.


Tier 2	
Bargain Hunter (Utility)	
Reduces the cost of equipment by a percentage.	Highly recommended.

Freya's effectiveness relies heavily on stacking Attack Speed items quickly. This talent accelerates her farm, allowing her to reach her critical item power spikes (Corrosion Scythe, DHS) faster than the enemy.


Tier 3 (Key Talent)
Killing Spree (Sustain/Burst)
Restores a percentage of HP and grants a significant burst of Movement Speed upon securing a kill or assist.

Core Talent. This talent provides the essential "reset" needed for Freya's dive potential. The instant HP recovery and speed boost allow her to survive retaliation after a kill, rapidly reposition, or escape to continue hunting the next target.



By choosing the Assassin Emblem with Thrill and Killing Spree, you emphasize a fast, aggressive, and rewarding playstyle:

Maximized Damage: Thrill ensures her damage is consistently high from the start.

Faster Scaling: Bargain Hunter ensures she gets her late-game items online quicker.

Unstoppable Momentum: Killing Spree ensures that once Freya starts getting kills, she becomes nearly unstoppable due to the continuous healing and speed boost.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Tough Boots / Warrior Boots

Movement/Defense

Tough Boots are usually the better choice for Freya. They reduce the duration of Crowd Control (CC) effects, which is her biggest weakness, allowing her to continue generating Orbs and using Skill 2 for mobility even when targeted.


2. War Axe

Sustained Damage

Core Item. Provides Physical Attack, a crucial HP boost, and an easily activated passive that grants stacking Physical Penetration and Movement Speed.


3. Corrosion Scythe

Attack Speed/Utility

Core Attack Speed Item. This is essential to compensate for her lower base Attack Speed post-revamp. It provides a massive Attack Speed boost, and its passive slows the enemy upon hitting them with a Basic Attack.


4. Demon Hunter Sword (DHS)

Attack Speed/Anti-Tank

Core Damage Item. This provides more Attack Speed and a passive that deals a percentage of the target's current HP as extra physical damage. This item is what allows Freya to melt high-HP targets (Tanks and Fighters) quickly, ensuring she remains relevant even against tanky compositions.


5. Oracle

Defense/Sustain

Core Defensive Item. Grants significant Magic Defense, but the true value is its passive, which increases Shield Absorption and HP Regeneration Effects by 30%.


6. Immortality / Brute Force

Late Game Defense

Immortality is the safest late-game choice, providing a second chance after a risky dive. Brute Force Breastplate is a strong alternative if you need more consistent tankiness and mobility, as its stacking defense and movement speed synergize with her constant spamming of Skill 2.

hero.hero_overview:
Passive: Power of Einherjar

Basic Attacks grant Freya a stack of Sacred Orb (max 6). When Freya has 3 or more Sacred Orbs, her next Basic Attack is enhanced with bonus Attack Speed and a damage increase for a short duration. Orbs are also consumed by her other skills.


Skill 1: Leap of Faith

Freya leaps to a target location, dealing damage and pulling enemies towards her. Consumes 3 Sacred Orbs to cast. This is her primary initiation and CC tool.


Skill 2: Valkyrie Slash

Freya dashes a short distance and enhances her next Basic Attack. The skill has no cooldown and can be cast consecutively, consuming 1 Sacred Orb each time. This is her key tool for constant mobility, damage, and sustained fighting.


Ultimate: Valkyrie Descent

Freya transforms into her Valkyrie form, flying into the designated area. Upon landing, she deals damage, gains a significant shield, and her Basic Attacks are enhanced to deal ranged AoE Splash Damage for a duration.
hero.hero_strength:
Exceptional Mobility: Skill 2 allows for rapid, consecutive dashes, making her incredibly hard to lock down and excellent for chasing or escaping.

High Sustained Damage: Her Passive and Skill 2 allow for continuous damage output and trading, especially when combined with Attack Speed items.

Backline Threat (Ultimate): Her Ultimate grants her ranged splash damage, allowing her to easily dive the backline and threaten multiple squishy heroes simultaneously.

Good Laning/Jungle Clear: Her Skill 2 enhances Basic Attacks, making quick work of minions and jungle creeps.
hero.hero_weakness:
CC Vulnerability: Freya is still highly susceptible to chain Crowd Control (CC) effects, which can shut down her ability to generate Sacred Orbs and use her mobility.

Orb Dependency: Her mobility and burst potential are entirely dependent on having Sacred Orbs. If she can't hit enemies to generate orbs, her effectiveness drops sharply.

Vulnerable During Initiation: If she uses Leap of Faith (S1) without Ultimate up, she can be easily focused and burst down before she can sustain the fight.

data.recommend: The Revamped Freya is a sustained damage powerhouse whose strength lies in her relentless mobility. To truly dominate, players must master Sacred Orb management and execute the ultimate backline dive.

spell.spell_desc:
A versatile spell for utility, defense, and advanced initiation.

While Freya has high natural mobility with Skill 2, Flicker is used for two main reasons: 1. Advanced Combos: You can use your Ultimate, then Flicker into a perfect backline position. 2. Escape/Repositioning: It allows you to instantly escape heavy Crowd Control (CC) or jump a wall when your Skill 2 dashes are not enough. Usage: Excellent for a crucial, unmissable initiation (S1 > Flicker to adjust position > Ultimate) or as a defensive measure to escape when your shield from the Ultimate runs out.

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Tigreal: The Perfect Frontline Partner for Freya

Tigreal is one of the strongest tank initiators in MLBB, and he pairs exceptionally well with Freya due to his ability to group enemies, disrupt formations, and create the ideal environment for Freya to dive in.

Why Tigreal Is Highly Compatible:

Tigreal can start fights with Initiate then Flicker then Implosion, pulling multiple enemies together. This sets up Freya to follow with her Ultimate landing slow, Skill 1 knockup, and multiple Skill 2 slashes.

His Shield and CC uptime keeps enemies locked long enough for Freya's Orb-based combos to deal maximum damage.

When Freya jumps into a fight, she often becomes the primary target. Tigreal's peel mechanics (Slow, Taunt, Knockback) help keep damage off her, allowing her to stay in Valkyrie mode longer and heal through basic attacks.

Tigreal also enhances Freya's mid-game teamfight dominance. When Tigreal hits a multi-man ultimate, Freya can clean up entire teams fast due to splash damage from Valkyrie Descent.

Angela: The Best Sustain and Damage Amplifier for Freya

Angela transforms Freya into an unstoppable bruiser. When Angela casts her Ultimate on Freya, the synergy becomes overwhelming for the enemy team.

Why Angela Is Highly Compatible:

Freya's kit already includes shields, lifesteal, and continuous damage. Angela adds healing, movement speed buffs, shields, and additional damage, turning Freya into a hyper-carry diver.

Angela's Heartguard allows Freya to dive backlines without fear of burst damage, making her more durable during her Valkyrie state.

Angela's Skill 1 slows enemies heavily, enabling Freya to stick to elusive targets like Marksmen and mages who would normally kite her.

Angela's Skill 2 adds extra damage, further enhancing Freya's DPS during orb-enhanced combos.

counters.counter_desc:
Khufra: The Ultimate Anti-Dive Stopper

Khufra is one of the strongest counters to Freya because his entire kit is designed to shut down heroes who rely on jumping, dashing, or diving into the backline. Freya's Skill 1 (Leap of Faith) and her Ultimate dive both become extremely risky when a Khufra is present.

Why Khufra Counters Freya:

Khufra's Bouncing Ball stops dashes and leaps, which means Freya cannot reliably engage or escape once he activates this skill.

When Freya tries to jump in, Khufra simply transforms into Bouncing Ball and locks her in place, preventing her from chaining Skill 2 effectively.

His skill kit has multiple CC layers (knock-ups, slows, immobilizations), which Freya hates because she is very vulnerable to CC. Her Orbs and DPS mean nothing if she cannot move or attack.

Khufra's Furious Strikes and Ultimate create a control zone that prevents Freya from reaching enemy backlines, forcing her to fight in an unfavorable position.

Franco: Hard Suppression That Completely Stops Freya's Combo

Franco is one of the most brutal counters to Freya because of his single target suppression, which completely disables her ability to survive long fights or activate Valkyrie Descent's sustain effect.

Why Franco Counters Freya:

One Iron Hook can start the downfall of Freya. Because she often plays aggressively and stands closer to enemy lines, she becomes a frequent target for Franco hooks.

His Brutal Massacre (Ultimate) is a suppression, meaning Freya cannot heal, cannot move, cannot cast skills, and cannot use Orbs during the lock. This removes her survivability and makes her an easy kill.

If Freya tries to jump into the backline, Franco can instantly lock her down before she even begins her combo.

Franco's presence forces Freya to play more carefully, reducing her ability to pressure lanes and initiating fights.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game (Levels 1-5)

Use Leap of Faith (Skill 1) to engage or farm. The leap gives you two Sacred Orbs on cast, so use it early to stack orbs for future fights.

Focus on wave-clearing carefully — don't dive too deep yet because your orb regeneration is still low. Use your empowered basic attacks (once you have orbs) to harass enemies safely.

If you run out of orbs, go back or play more passively. Staying too long without them makes you weak and vulnerable.

Prioritize taking small skirmishes rather than full fights. When both orbs and HP are favorable, you can trade aggressively; otherwise, back off and stack for later.


Mid Game (Levels 6-11)

Once you have at least a few orbs and a couple of core items (attack speed / penetration / sustain), start rotating to side lanes or join team fights. Your mobility from Valkyrie Slash (Skill 2) lets you dive or reposition quickly.

In fights, look for opportunities to leap in with Leap of Faith + Slash combo, then follow up with empowered basic attacks powered by your orbs. Your burst and sustained damage make you very dangerous in 1v1 or 2v2 engagements.

Use your Ultimate Valkyrie Descent strategically: target grouped enemies or high-value squishy heroes. When you land and enter Valkyrie Mode, your basic attacks become ranged and heal you, so maintain pressure.

Don't be afraid to trade health for value — your sustain from orbs + healing in Valkyrie Mode can often let you win small skirmishes.


Late Game (Levels 12+)

In late-game team fights, you are a diver / skirmisher. Wait for your frontline to engage, then dive in with Leap of Faith + Slash, using orbs to enhance your damage.

Make use of Valkyrie Mode from your ultimate to deal splash damage, heal, and avoid being locked down with CC immunity. Position well so you can hit multiple targets.

Target high-damage backline enemies (mages or marksmen) after they're softened. Your burst + sustained damage can quickly eliminate key threats.

1. Master Sacred Orb Management (Freya's Core Mechanic)

Freya's entire power spikes, combos, and damage output revolve around properly managing Sacred Orbs, so treat them like a resource equal to mana or energy.


2. Treat Leap of Faith (Skill 1) as Versatile — Not Just Engage

Skill 1 is more than a jump: it is Freya's shield, mobility, and crowd control tool. Use it to initiate, escape, or dodge crucial skills.


3. Always Open With Ultimate for Bigger Impact

Freya's ultimate gives her splash damage, extra fixed damage, slow effect, and lifesteal.


4. Maintain Aggressive Momentum, but Don't Overcommit

Freya is a dive hero, but she can be punished if you dive without backup. Engage only when your tank or roamer is nearby to soak some damage or follow your initiation.


5. Chain Skill 2 Properly — Don't Spam Mindlessly

Skill 2 is spammable but requires 2 Orbs per cast. Cast Skill 2 only when you can follow up with basic attacks, ensuring you regain orb efficiency.


6. Abuse Her Lifesteal During Valkyrie State

In Valkyrie mode, your basic attacks heal you for a significant amount.


7. Positioning Is Key — Come From the Side or Backline

Never start fights from the front; you will get CC'd immediately.


8. Assume You're the Main Damage Diver — Time Your Entry

Freya is a bruiser who acts like a semi-assassin. Let your tank initiate first. Enter half a second after your tank's CC lands — this ensures you get maximum uptime on enemies.


9. Always Track Key Enemy CC Before Diving

Freya loses most fights when she is stunned or suppressed mid-dive. Keep track of dangerous skills: Franco hook, Khufra bounce, Valir push, Kaja ultimate, etc.


10. Choose Targets Wisely

As Freya, your goal is to create chaos by removing enemy backliners quickly.
Prioritize MM > Mage > Assassin > Fighter > Tank


11. Manage Your Defensive CDs (Shield Timing)

The shield from Skill 1 is crucial for surviving early parts of fights.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin Emblem: Rupture, Weapon Master, War Cry

This emblem setup is currently the strongest and most updated choice for Revamp Freya, especially after her reworked kit shifted her playstyle toward fast engages, empowered basic attacks, and burst-heavy skirmishes. The Assassin Emblem enhances her ability to dive backliners, secure kills quickly, and reposition safely thanks to the increased movement and attack speed she gains during combat.

Tier 1 Talent: Rupture

Rupture provides Physical Penetration, which significantly boosts the damage of Freya's empowered basic attacks and skill-based burst. With her new Sacred Orb system, she frequently relies on short trading windows where she must maximize damage output quickly. Rupture lets her cut through early armor values, helping her win lane duels, punish mispositioned heroes, and maintain strong early pressure in the EXP lane or jungle.

Tier 2 Talent: Weapon Master

Weapon Master enhances Freya's scaling by increasing the bonus Physical Attack she gains from her items. Since Freya's revamp allows her to chain empowered basic attacks very quickly, every point of Physical Attack gained through items becomes more valuable. This talent makes her mid and late-game power spike much stronger, as her damage increases at a faster rate compared to other emblem setups. It also gives her extra value from offensive items such as Windtalker, Corrosion Scythe, or Berserker's Fury.

Tier 3 Talent: War Cry

War Cry is the core of this entire emblem setup. After triggering damage on enemies, it grants a large Attack Speed boost that perfectly synergizes with Freya's kit. Her Sacred Orbs allow her to enhance basic attacks in rapid succession, and War Cry multiplies the speed of her combos. This makes her chase potential, split-push speed, and teamfight DPS significantly higher. War Cry provides constant pressure once she enters a fight, allowing her to shred squishy heroes almost instantly.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Warrior Boots

Provides physical defense and movement speed, allowing Freya to both initiate fights and survive early exchanges. 

This helps her take advantage of her mobility and maintain presence in the EXP lane. 



2. Windtalker

Grants +40% Attack Speed, +20% Movement Speed, and 10% Crit Chance. 

Synergizes well with her Sacred Orb passive, as the extra attack speed lets her ramp up DPS when orbs are ready.



3. Endless Battle

Offers +65 Physical Attack, +250 HP, +10% Cooldown Reduction, +15% Lifesteal, and a passive "Divine Justice" which adds true damage after using a skill. 

The lifesteal, true damage, and cooldown reduction are very valuable for Freya's sustained fighting style.



4. Blade of Despair (BOD)

High Physical Attack to amplify her burst potential when she engages. 

This item complements her ability to dive in with orbs and deal massive damage quickly.



5. Great Dragon Spear

Adds Physical Attack, Critical Chance, and Cooldown Reduction.
Helps her spam abilities faster, making her orb usage and skill-based combo more fluid.



6. Queen's Wings

Provides +1000 HP, +15 Physical Attack, and a passive that reduces received damage when her health is low, plus additional lifesteal. 

Excellent for survivability in team fights, allowing her to dive into enemy teams and sustain through pressure.



7. Immortality (Situational / Late Game)

Gives HP and Physical Defense, plus a resurrection passive to give her a second life in critical moments. 

Useful when she is a key damage-dealer or front-line diver, as it provides insurance in late-game teamfights.

hero.hero_overview:
Background / Lore
With her latest update, Freya fully embraces her role as a Valkyrie—an ethereal warrior inspired by Norse mythology. The revamp reintroduces her as a fierce, mobile fighter whose power flows through her accumulation of Sacred Orbs. She channels the spirit of the Einherjar (fallen warriors), using their strength to slash through her enemies and sustain in battle with divine resilience. Her new design and animations highlight her status as a noble warrior, combining valor, aerial grace, and raw physical might. 

Revamp & Release

The revamped Freya (Valkyrie) was officially released on November 5, 2025. 

Her visual redesign draws from her Valkyrie archetype, featuring Norse-inspired armor and celestial motifs to reflect her status as a battle-borne guardian. 


Role & Specialty

Role: Fighter

Specialty: High mobility, sustained DPS, dive / skirmish

Primary Use: EXP lane or Jungle

With her revamp, Freya becomes more flexible: able to chase, burst, and sustain, rather than being purely a tanky bruiser.


Skills (Revamped)

Passive: Sacred Orb (Power of Einherjar)
Freya now stores up to 6 Sacred Orbs, which regenerate automatically when she's out of combat. 
Each orb consumed boosts her next basic attack with an attack-speed bonus, making her more dangerous when she dives into fights. 

Skill 1: Leap of Faith
Freya leaps forward and smashes the ground with her shield, dealing physical damage to nearby enemies and knocking them airborne for a short duration. 
Additionally, using this ability grants her two Sacred Orbs, which directly fuels her offensive potential. 

Skill 2: Valkyrie Slash
This is now a core part of her kit: Freya can spam this dash/slash as long as she has Sacred Orbs (no base cooldown). 
When she consumes orbs, each cast increases her attack speed significantly, allowing for rapid, continuous attacks. 

Ultimate: Valkyrie Descent
Freya dives into a target area, dealing area physical damage and slowing enemies on impact.
hero.hero_strength:
1. High Mobility and Aggressive Dive Potential
With her revamped kit, Freya becomes much more mobile. Her Skill 2 (Valkyrie Slash) can be cast repeatedly as long as she has "Sacred Orbs," which makes her extremely flexible in fights. This lets her dash in and out of combat quickly, chasing squishy targets or disengaging when things go wrong. According to guides, this mobility gives her a much stronger presence in skirmishes than before. 


2. Sustained Damage + Burst Hybrid
Freya's passive allows her to store up to six Sacred Orbs, which significantly boost her next basic attack's speed.  She isn't just a pure burst champion anymore, she can both dive in for a quick combo and maintain consistent damage output as her orbs regenerate. This makes her very deadly in 1v1 duels and also in extended team fights.


3. Improved Survivability and Lifesteal
Her ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) grants her a shield when she lands and, in her Valkyrie state, her basic attacks heal her based on her physical attack.  This makes her more sustainable in fights: she can dive, deal damage, and recover, rather than relying solely on burst or being too squishy.


4. CC Immunity During Key Moments
In her Ultimate, Freya gains crowd control immunity while she dives in for her descent.  This allows her to avoid being interrupted mid-engagement, making her initiation more reliable and dangerous.


5. Strong Duel and Split-Push Potential
Thanks to her orb mechanic and enhanced basic attack speed, Freya can win duels effectively. She scales very well in 1v1 matchups because the more she fights, the more orbs she builds, and the faster and stronger she becomes.  Also, her mobility and sustain make her capable of applying pressure in side lanes, rotating to pick off targets or escape when needed.


6. Strategic Depth and Flexibility
The revamp gives Freya more tactical options: she can build for burst, sustain or a balanced playstyle.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. Squishier Than Expected
While Freya's revamp gives her more mobility and burst, she loses the high durability she once had. Several players note that despite new sustain tools, she still feels fragile and can be quickly shut down by crowd control. 


2. Reliance on Sacred Orbs
Her new mechanics depend heavily on Sacred Orbs. If she spends all her orbs, her damage and attack-speed potential drop significantly, making her more predictable and easier to punish. Generating orbs requires time out of combat, which can be risky when pressured. 


3. Less Effective Without Boosted Second Skill
According to her rework, her Skill 2 (Valkyrie Slash) becomes much weaker if not enhanced by her first skill, meaning poor orb management can severely limit her combat performance. 


4. Reduced Shield From Ultimate
Her new ultimate gives a heal and ranged attacks, but the shield she used to get from her old ultimate is significantly reduced in the revamp. This makes her more vulnerable during her initial dive and less tanky during fights. 


5. Vulnerable to Crowd Control
Despite having CC immunity during her ultimate jump, she can be heavily punished before or after that window. Fighters or mages with high CC can lock her down when her mobility or orb-boosted states are unavailable. 


6. High Skill Floor / Learning Curve
The new playstyle demands strong orb management, precise timing of skills, and smart decision-making. Many players feel she's become more difficult to master compared to her older, more straightforward kit.

data.recommend: Revamped Freya performs best when played as an aggressive mid-to-late game diver who manages her Sacred Orbs carefully before committing to fights.

spell.spell_desc:
Inspire
Inspire is often considered the best battle spell for Freya in her revamped form. Inspire boosts her attack speed and gives bonus penetration, which synergizes really well with her Sacred Orb mechanic and her enhanced basic attacks. 

Why it Works Well:

When Freya uses her orbs (from her passive), her basic attacks become faster — Inspire magnifies this effect, making her burst and sustained DPS much stronger. 

It helps her clear waves and trade more efficiently in the early and mid game, since she can deal more damage per second.

In duels or skirmishes, the increased attack speed from Inspire helps her repeatedly use her enhanced attacks and stay threatening as long as she has orbs.

Many Freya players and guides recommend Inspire because it fits her "fighter / bruiser" identity after revamp, giving her both damage and sustained fights.


Alternative Option: Flicker
If you prefer a more aggressive or playmaking style, Flicker is also very viable. Freya's revamped kit gives her good engage potential with Leap of Faith (Skill 1), and combining that with Flicker lets her dive in or reposition mid-fight more safely.


Use Inspire if you want to maximize DPS and stay strong in duels or long trades.

Use Flicker if you want to play aggressively, make surprise engages, or dive into backlines.

hero.hero_overview:
Freya, the Valkyrie,  is a Fighter hero in Mobile Legends Bangbang (MLBB) who specializes in Charge/Burst damage. She is #22 hero in MLBB which was initially released in November 2016. The revamped Freya was recently released on November 5, 2025. Her revamp has given her the ability to constantly use her dash skill and ultimate to deal massive AoE (Area of Effect) damage, making her a formidable initiator and damage dealer in team fights. She relies heavily on her Sacred Orb passive stacks to chain her skills. 


⚡[ FREYA'S SKILLS ]

✨[ Passive Skill ] Power of Einherjar: Her Basic Attacks grant Sacred Orbs (max 6 stacks) and also grant a temporary Attack Speed boost after casting any skill, encouraging Basic Attack weaving. Sacred Orbs fuel her Skill 2 (Spirit Combo), allowing consecutive dashes without cooldown.

✨[ SKILL 1 ] - Leap of Faith: Freya leaps to a target area, dealing damage and briefly pulling enemies towards the center, which is excellent for initiation and isolating a target.

✨[ SKILL 2 ] - Valkyrie Slash: Freya dashes forward, dealing damage, and slowing enemies. This skill has no cooldown but consumes 2 Sacred Orbs per cast.

✨[ ULTIMATE ] - Valkyrie Descent: Freya dives down to the target which slows the enemies in her path and enters the Valkyrie state. Her basic attacks deal splash damage with bonus damage and recover HP for 12 seconds.


⚡[ SKILL LEVEL PRIORITY SUGGESTION ]:

1. Max the Skill 2 FIRST. 
2. Always upgrade ULT immediately at Level 4, 8, and 12.
3. Get Skill 1 at level 2 and Max it at the LAST part.

Lvl 5-15: Prioritize ULT > S2 > S1 


⚡[ SKILL COMBOS ]:
Freya's main strength is her ability to constantly use her Skill 2, so the key is always to manage your Sacred Orb stacks by Basic attacks

1. Ganking/Chase: S2 > Basic Atk (1-2x) > S2 > Basic Atk >S2
2. Initiation/ Burst: S1 > S2 > S2 > ULT > S2> Ranged basic atks
3. Instant Teamfights: ULT > S1 > S2 (3x) > S2 > Ranged basic atks
hero.hero_strength:
Here are the main strengths that Revamped Freya brings: 

1. High Sustained Mobility and Damage: Her Skill 2 combined with her passive allows for constant, low-cooldown dashes. This makes her excellent at chasing down low-HP targets and repositioning during fights.

2. Strong Teamfight Initiation and Area of Effect (AoE) Control: Her Ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) gives her an instant power spike with a massive shield and turns her Basic Attacks into ranged splash damage, allowing her to easily dive the backline and hit multiple enemies.

3. Effective Shield and Sustain: She gains significant shields from her Ultimate and her Skill 2 dashes (upon max stacks) which scale with her Physical Attack, making her surprisingly tanky when diving a full team.

4. Excellent Turret Damage: Her high Attack Speed and Physical Attack scaling, especially when combined with the Inspire spell, allow her to melt turrets quickly, making her a formidable split-pusher and objective taker.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. Extremely Vulnerable to Hard Crowd Control (CC): Her reliance on chaining skills (S2 dashes) and Basic Attacks makes her highly susceptible to heroes who can stun, suppress, or interrupt her (e.g., Minsitthar's Ultimate, Kaja's Ultimate, or Chou's Ultimate). If she is locked down mid-combo, she dies easily.

2. Weak Against Anti-Shield/Anti-Heal: Heroes or items that punish shield/sustain (like Esmeralda or Sea Halberd) greatly reduce her durability, as her survivability relies heavily on her shields and lifesteal.

3. Orb Management Dependent: Her potential drops sharply if she cannot maintain her Sacred Orb stacks (by weaving Basic Attacks between skills). If the team fight is chaotic and she can't hit a target, she runs out of dashes and becomes a sitting duck.

4. Item Dependent: Freya needs her core damage and attack speed items (like War Axe and Corrosion Scythe) to reach her power spike. She can feel quite weak and squishy in the early game compared to other EXP laners if she falls behind in gold.

data.recommend: Master the Dash, Dive, and Dominate the Battlefield with the Revamped Valkyrie!

The talents give her extra damage, which makes her big Ultimate shield even stronger, and more importantly, she gets constant healing from the Festival of Blood talent every time she hits an enemy with a skill. Best of all, the main talent, Brave Smite, gives her a free mini-shield every single time she uses her stun or knock-up skills, making her nearly impossible to stop when she dives into the enemy team.

equips.equip_desc: Freya builds items that mix high Physical Damage with crucial Defense to survive her aggressive dives. Start with Tough Boots for protection against stuns. Her main power comes from Blade of Despair (for high attack) and Endless Battle (for healing and True Damage). You must include Oracle because it makes the protective shield from her Ultimate much, much larger. Finish the build with defensive items like Dominance Ice (to stop enemy healing/speed) and Malefic Roar to melt down tanks.

hero.hero_overview: Freya is a fighter who is like a flying, electric wrecking ball—she is tough and hits very hard! You play her in the side lane (EXP Lane) or in the jungle. Her main goal is to use her ultimate power-up to become invincible for a moment, fly over to the enemy's weakest heroes (like the shooters or mages), and knock them up and quickly defeat them. She needs to keep attacking to get special "Orbs" to make her skills stronger, so she is always running forward and hitting things.
hero.hero_strength: Freya is amazing because she is a bully early in the game. She can beat almost anyone in a one-on-one fight right away. When she uses her Ultimate (her biggest skill), she gets a massive shield that lets her jump right under enemy towers without dying. She has lots of jumps and knock-up skills, which means she can easily chase down fleeing enemies and stop them from escaping or attacking your team. She is perfect for rushing the enemy and starting the fight for your team.
hero.hero_weakness: The biggest problem with Freya is that she is useless when her Ultimate is cooling down—she becomes very weak and easy to kill. She can also be stopped easily by heroes who have long-lasting Stuns or Cages (called "Hard CC"). If you get hit by a long stun, her shield melts, and she dies before she can fight back. Also, if you run out of her special "Orbs," her skills become weak, so you always have to be careful not to waste them or miss your attacks.

Blade of Despair

Malefic Roar

This combination maximizes her basic attack/passive synergy, true damage, and overall physical attack for both damage and survivability.

hero.hero_overview:
The Revamped Freya, the Valkyrie, has undergone a significant transformation, moving from a pure burst/attack speed fighter to a highly mobile, team-fight-oriented Valkyrie General who specializes in area control and sustained damage.

Revamped Skills Overview:

Passive: Power of Einherjar
Freya gains 1 Sacred Orb with each Basic Attack (up to 6 Sacred Orbs). Consuming 1 Sacred Orb term grants Freya 100% Attack Speed for her next Basic Attack (max 6 stacks).
Sacred Orbs gradually recover when out of combat.

Skill1: Leap of Faith
Freya leaps to the target area and strikes the ground with her shield, gaining 500 (+100% Total Physical Attack) shield and dealing 200 (+80% Extra Physical Attack) Physical Damage to enemies within while knocking them airborne for 0.4s.

Skill 2: Valkyrie Slash
Freya charges forward and slashes, dealing 150 (+50% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and slowing enemies by 30% for 0.5s. This skill has no base cooldown but consumes 2 Sacred Orbs per cast.
This skill deals 50% damage to Creeps.

Skill 3: Valkyrie Descent
Freya selects a target area and dives down as a Sacred Orb, dealing 300 (+75% Total Physical Attack) Physical Damage and 40% slow to enemies in her path and at the landing point for 2s. Upon landing, Freya enters the Valkyrie state for 12s, during which her Basic Attacks deal 30 extra damage (extra damage cannot Crit) and she recovers 60 (+40% Total Physical Attack) HP per Basic Attack, and her Basic Attacks deal splash damage.
hero.hero_strength:
Exceptional Mobility & Chase
Her Skill 2 (Valkyrie Slash) now has no cooldown and can be cast continuously by spending Sacred Orbs, giving her unmatched dash spam and chase potential, similar to an Assassin.

Massive Team Fight Impact
Her Ultimate, Valkyrie Descent, allows her to initiate from long range (similar to a global-range blink), land with a shield, and transform her Basic Attacks into ranged AoE damage. This allows her to disrupt the enemy backline and clear multiple enemies simultaneously.

High Outplay Potential
The combination of her spammable dashes (S2), her new shield-toss CC (S1), and her enhanced basic attacks gives skilled players immense control over positioning and damage focus.

Unstoppable Objective Take
While in her Ultimate form, the ranged AoE basic attacks allow her to quickly clear jungle creeps and push turrets from a safer distance.

Built-in Sustain
Her Ultimate grants a shield upon activation and can include healing in its transformed state (depending on the specific revamp patch/talents), making her highly durable in extended fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Reliance on Sacred Orbs
Her entire mobility and damage amplification are tied to her Sacred Orb passive. If she is unable to land attacks or skills to generate Orbs, her damage output plummets, and her S2 dashes are disabled.

Slower Attack Speed Compared to Old Kit
The attack speed bonus from her old passive has been significantly reduced or entirely tied to Orb generation, making her early-game farming and sustained DPS feel sluggish without certain core items like Golden Staff or Corrosion Scythe.

Vulnerable to Hard CC
Despite the increased mobility, if Freya is hit by a strong, unavoidable Crowd Control (CC) skill (e.g., Minsitthar's Ult, Franco's Ult) while deep in the enemy line, her life-saving dash sequence is interrupted, making her an easy target.

High Item Dependency (Scaling)
She needs 2-3 core items (like War Axe, Golden Staff, or a Crit item) to reach her full damage and speed potential. She can be easily pressured and shut down in the early game before her first items are completed.

Ultimate Commitment
While her Ultimate provides great initiation, it's a one-way trip. Once she flies in, she is often isolated and must rely on her S2 dashes and shield to survive the focus fire.

hero.hero_overview:
Freya is a Fighter/Bruiser who excels with basic-attack spam, multiple knock-ups, and a powerful lifesteal-boosting ultimate. After her revamp, she becomes a highly aggressive, mobile, and snowball-oriented fighter who relies on Sacred Orbs to spam her Skill 2 without a base cooldown.
She shines in duels, skirmishes, and extended teamfights with her Valkyrie State enabling massive sustain and AoE damage.
hero.hero_strength:
1. Unlimited Skill 2 Spam (Orb-Based)
Her Skill 2 has no base cooldown — as long as she has Sacred Orbs, she can burst enemies quickly.
2. High Lifesteal During Ultimate
In Valkyrie State, Freya gains splash basic attacks and strong lifesteal, making her hard to kill in teamfights.
3. Great Mobility
Her Skill 1 dash and empowered Skill 2 jump help her chase, engage, or re-position easily.
4. Extremely Strong in 1v1 and Small Skirmishes
Her combination of knock-ups, burst, and sustain makes her a dominant duelist from mid to late game.
5. Amazing Snowball Potential
Once ahead, Freya becomes very difficult to stop and can solo pressure lanes or dive backlines freely.
6. Reliable Crowd Control
Both Skill 1 and empowered Skill 2 provide knock-up, giving her excellent single-target lockdown.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. Heavily Dependent on Sacred Orbs
Without orbs, she loses access to Skill 2 spam, making her ineffective during extended fights or when forced to retreat.
2. Weaker Early Game
Her early clear speed was nerfed, and she needs items to become truly threatening.
3. Vulnerable to Heavy CC
Stun, suppression, and long-duration control can completely shut her down before she can lifesteal or burst.
4. Must Dive Into Enemy Formation
Freya plays as a frontliner/diver, making her the focus target in many fights — needs defensive items to survive.
5. Ultimate Timing Is Crucial
Misusing her ultimate or diving without proper setup can cause her to die instantly or waste her Valkyrie State.

hero.hero_overview:
Hero Overview

Freya, the Valkyrie, is a powerful fighter who excels in close-range combat with her enhanced basic attacks and strong shield mechanics. After her rework, she now has more consistent damage output, smoother skill chaining, and better sustain in team fights.

She dominates the EXP Lane, capable of dueling most fighters and snowballing after her first core item. Freya's skills give her both burst and sustained damage, making her dangerous in both 1v1 and team engagements.

Freya's combo revolves around skill-enhanced Basic Attacks and timing her ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) for shield and burst potential. She scales Incredibly well into mid-late game, especially once she secures her core items like War Axe and Dominance Ice.

She fits best in compositions that allow her to dive the backline while allies provide peel or follow-up CC.
hero.hero_strength:
Hero Advantages

1. Strong Initiation Ability

Her first and third skills are both mobility skills, allowing her to quickly enter the enemy formation or lock onto the backline. At the right moment, she can force the enemy to initiate a team fight or disrupt their formation, creating opportunities for her team.

2. High Attack Speed, High Output

Her passive ability grants her high attack speed on her next two basic attacks after casting a skill, making her extremely threatening in close combat.

Her damage comes not only from skills but also from the combination of basic attacks and skills. With proper execution, she can deliver strong sustained damage.

3. Shield, Survivability

During her ultimate, she gains a shield, and her basic attacks deal area-of-effect damage.

This gives her good survivability after engaging, preventing her from being instantly killed.
hero.hero_weakness:
Hero Weaknesses

1. Vulnerable to Control and Pulling Away

While she has the ability to leap in, Freya may be at a disadvantage when close to the enemy if they have strong control or suppression abilities.

2. Reliant on Items and Timing

She needs proper item support to fully utilize her passive + auto-attack + skill combo. Without items or when behind in gold, her threat is significantly reduced.

3. High Requirement for Teamfight Coordination

While she can be strong in lane, in high-level games or teamfights, her primary role is to take down the enemy mage or marksman, not simply to tank damage. Without team coordination, support, or protection, she may become isolated. Her lack of ranged damage and need for close-range combat makes her vulnerable against tightly packed enemy formations or well-protected backlines.

4. Lack of Consecutive Damage Output

Her strength in lane is diminished against heroes who excel at poke, ranged harassment, and have abundant control.

hero.hero_overview:
Freya is a fighter hero who excels at burst damage and sustained fighting. Her revamp has made her more mobile and adaptable, allowing her to be played in various roles, though she shines brightest in the EXP lane or as a jungler. She relies heavily on stacking her Sacred Orb stacks to unleash her full potential.

Abilities
 
- Passive - Power of Einherjar: Freya gains a Sacred Orb each time she deals damage to enemies with her Basic Attacks or skills (up to 4 stacks). Each stack of Sacred Orb increases her Attack Speed. When Sacred Orb is fully stacked, Freya enters Enhanced State.

- Skill 1 - Leap of Faith: Freya leaps in the specified direction, dealing Physical Damage to enemies hit and pulling them towards her. Enhanced State: Freya knocks enemies Airborne and deals extra Physical Damage.

- Skill 2 - Spirit Combo: Freya launches a series of attacks forward, each dealing Physical Damage. Enhanced State: Freya summons a shield, gaining extra Physical Defense, and deals extra Physical Damage.

- Ultimate - Valkyrie Descent: Freya transforms into Valkyrie, gaining extra Physical Attack, Magic Defense, and Control Immunity for a duration.
hero.hero_strength:
- High Burst Damage: Freya can deal massive burst damage, especially when she has her Sacred Orb stacks fully charged. This makes her effective at quickly eliminating squishy targets.

- Good Mobility: With "Leap of Faith," Freya has decent mobility for initiating fights, chasing enemies, or escaping danger.

- Sustain: Her second skill in Enhanced State grants a shield that can help her sustain in battles, making her difficult to take down quickly.

- Control Immunity: Her ultimate provides control immunity, allowing her to dive into team fights without being easily interrupted.

- Versatile: She can be played in multiple roles, adapting to the needs of the team.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Reliance on Stacks: Freya's damage is significantly reduced without her Sacred Orb stacks. She needs time to build them up before being effective.

- Vulnerable to CC: While her ultimate grants control immunity, she is vulnerable to crowd control effects when it's on cooldown or before she activates it.

- Mana Dependent: Freya can be mana-hungry, especially in the early game. Managing her mana is crucial.

- Weak Early Game: Freya's early game is relatively weak compared to other fighters. She needs to farm and scale up to become a threat.

- Countered by Anti-Heal: Since her sustain relies on her shield, heroes with anti-heal effects can reduce her effectiveness in team fights.

hero.hero_overview:
Revamped Freya Skills

Passive — Power of Einherjar

Freya gains one Rune stack each time she deals damage to an enemy hero. After reaching 4 stacks, the Rune activates, increasing Freya's Movement Speed by 30 and empowering her Basic Attack to deal extra damage and apply a crowd control effect.

Skill 1 — Leap of Faith

Freya leaps to the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area and pulling them toward the center.

Skill 2 — Spirit Combo

Freya gains a shield and performs 3 quick strikes forward. Each strike deals damage and slows enemies.

Ultimate — Valkyrie Descent

Freya enters Valkyrie mode, gaining increased Attack Speed, Physical Attack, and Magic Defense. Her skills are also enhanced while in this mode.
hero.hero_strength:
- High Burst Damage: With the combination of her skills and passive, Freya is capable of dealing very high burst damage to enemies, especially when in Valkyrie mode. 

- Good Mobility: Skill 1 (Leap of Faith) provides good mobility for chasing enemies or escaping danger. 

- Crowd Control: Skill 1 also has a crowd control effect that pulls enemies to the center, allowing Freya or her team to control their movements. 

- Strong Dueling Ability: Freya is very strong in one-on-one combat, thanks to the shield from Skill 2 and the attribute boost from her Ultimate. 

- Adaptable: Freya's item build can be adjusted to suit the team's needs and match conditions.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Dependent on Passive: Freya's effectiveness is highly dependent on having her passive rune active. If it's not active, her damage and crowd control effects will be reduced. 

- Mana-Hosting: Freya has issues with mana consumption, especially when using skills continuously. 

- Vulnerable to Crowd Control: Despite having a shield, Freya is still vulnerable to crowd control effects like stuns and knock-ups, especially when in Valkyrie mode. 

- Weak Early Game: Freya tends to be weak in the early game and requires items to become strong. 

- Poor Positioning is Fatal: When using her Ultimate, Freya must be careful with her positioning as she can become an easy target if she moves too far forward.

hero.hero_overview:
Freya, the Valkyrie, is a powerful fighter who excels in close-range combat with her enhanced basic attacks and strong shield mechanics. After her rework, she now has more consistent damage output, smoother skill chaining, and better sustain in team fights.
She dominates the EXP Lane, capable of dueling most fighters and snowballing after her first core item. Freya's skills give her both burst and sustained damage, making her dangerous in both 1v1 and team engagements.

Freya's combo revolves around skill-enhanced Basic Attacks and timing her ultimate (Valkyrie Descent) for shield and burst potential. She scales incredibly well into mid-late game, especially once she secures her core items like War Axe and Dominance Ice.

She fits best in compositions that allow her to dive the backline while allies provide peel or follow-up CC.
hero.hero_strength:
Excellent Duelist: Freya can win most 1v1 matchups in the EXP lane due to her shield, lifesteal, and fast skill rotation.

Strong Snowball Potential: Once she gets her first item, she can continuously pressure side lanes and force rotations.

Flexible Playstyle: Can build both semi-tank or full fighter depending on team needs.

Good Teamfight Presence: Her Ultimate grants AoE damage and shield, allowing her to dive into the enemy backline safely.

Crowd Control Utility: Freya's Skill 2 (Leap of Faith) provides reliable airborne CC that can interrupt dashes or channeling abilities.

Great Sustain: Her passive and ultimate allow her to stay longer in fights and trade effectively.
hero.hero_weakness:
Vulnerable to CC: Despite her shield, Freya struggles when heavily crowd-controlled, especially against heroes like Franco or Akai.

Requires Items to Shine: Freya's early game before completing her first core item can be weak and punishable.

Short Range: She must commit fully to fights, and retreating after diving is difficult without team backup.

Mana Management: Early game mana consumption can limit her ability to trade or spam abilities.

Kited Easily: Ranged heroes like Esmeralda, Masha, or Thamuz can kite her if she misses her Leap of Faith.
