cooperates.cooperate_desc:
-Freya is a semi-tank character that relies on basic attack recovery. This concludes her vulnerability in crowd controls where she can't perform actions. 
-Angela aids her initial charge by providing shield on top of her Vengeance. This gives the opposing tank the dilemma of choosing to whether set earlier or later. Which either way is beneficial for Freya: Diminish their forces before CC or leave Freya open after CC.

-A situational occurrence of Alice Jungle pick. These two are the charge duo (one might say Phalanx) - Alice is the shield, while Freya is the spear.
-Alice would provide the CC and slows Freya would benefit for while sustaining frontal damages.
-Freya, would angle Alice from the rear and front by dealing damage.
-Marksman will love this strategy, as not only they are not prone to projectiles but is considerably safe.

-Freya is reluctant with exchanging damage for defense just to sustain incoming barrages inside crowd controls.
-Diggie resolves this issue by providing Freya the CC immunity she would gladly accept outside Purify or sustain items. The shield is a consolation.
-Diggie also annexes his skill to increase movement speed, increasing Freya's chasing capability whilst pinning down enemies.

counters.counter_desc:
-Reliant on skills that has blink in each one of them, Minsitthar is a nightmare for Freya.
-Minsitthars ultimate is the outmost definition of skill restriction for Freya. While Freya is vulnerable, on top of that, she can't use here skills.

-A situation where caution is everything.
-Obsidia, much like Minsitthar restricts Freya from accessing here skills. Aside from it, Obsidia is agile, which is a difficult prey to chase for Freya.
-While this may sound all good, once Obsidia enter Freya's range, she is automatically eradicated.

-Although as mentioned in the overview, Freya would have the upper hand against slow and squishy characters.
-Granger struggles to keep up with Freya's blinks and slow effect, derived from her items.
-Granger also struggle with scratching Freya at a safe range, without entering the danger zone. This is due to her mobility that lets her dodge projectiles.

-Arlott is a charge character that Freya preys on. His incapacity to disengage quickly allows Freya to land consistent basic attacks.
-Subtracting his teammates, Arlott with semi-tank build/pure damage build could never win 1 on 1 against Freya.
-Similar cases includes Phoveus, Pacquito, Dyrotth, and Ruby.
-Despite their mobilities, Yve, Harith, Pharsa, and Zhuxin would have a rough time escaping Freya once converted to a close quarrel.

-Your best partners are your worst rival. This concerns her mobility and damage.
-Angela always turns the tide by fortifying opponents, increasing their movement speed, and constricting Freya locked in place after the gradual slow.
-Freya almost always fails to catch-up to an Angela-augmented enemies.

dominants.dominant_desc:
-Scavenging for a reliable source of income, Freya is probably at her lowest in this stage. Overcome this stage without minion drought.
-Tips:
1) Always last hit minions if possible.
2) When enemy (especially strong early phase heroes) freezes the lane, try to at least close the range of minion to compensate loses. Use first skill to gain shield, sustain damage, and close the gap.
3) As much as possible, before the first minion hit the 1st opposing minion batch, cut the lane and interfere their jungle. Her early game is dominant compared to assassins.

-In this stage, Freya should have at least built 1-2, 3 if lucked. Freya is still squishy in this stage, so charge with caution.
-Tips:
1) Try to lock on squishy opponents. Upon their defeat, Freya should've suffered minimal damage.
2) Do not initiate the teamfight unless all teammates are present. Freya is a Valkyrie not a hero - engaging without teammates is a common mistake for Freya users.

-Upon purchasing the complete set, Freya is almost unstoppable.
-Tips:
1) Initiate team fight by charging unto the opposing's rear or their damage dealers.
2) Try to skillfully dodge crowd controls. Use Freya's blink skills.
3) Don't spam 2nd skill. Only use it when chasing opponent. Aside from not being able to perform basic attack, you are inclined with throwing away your attack speed items by not using them.
4) Spam 1st skill instead. Upon its cooldown restoration, immediately use it inside teamfight.

-Skill Combo:
1) Ultimate - Initial charge and slows the enemies for an easier landing of basic attack.
2) First Skill - Shield gain and stun plus closing range. An immediate reflex of marksman is to dodge and disallow close combats. First skill compromises this decision.
3) 2nd Skill to Basic Attack - Slight range closure. If the enemy marksman still have cards up their sleeves and decided to back out, Freya's 2nd skill also solves this. Now, that the opponent is in range, spam basic attack and recover damage whilst dealing damage.
-General Tip:
1) Don't be a hero and charge alone. Remember Freya is a semi-tank character, and is not meant to deal damage.
2) Always lean on opponents' rears. Flanking is the best and feasible word to describe her gameplay.
3) Always disrupt the opposing damage dealer.
4) Use basic attack more often than 2nd skill.
5) Don't engage without ultimate, Freya can't sustain and recover from that much damage without it.

emblems.emblem_desc:
-Fighter emblem is notably used for semi-tank heroes, which fits the frame of Freya's portrait. The adaptive attack from the fighter emblem is the highest bonus from all the emblems (tied with common emblem). This benefits her Ultimate.
-Swift entails more basic attack. This, paired with her new Ultimate, which recovers her HP for each basic attack landed (this disregards multiple targets) aids her sustainability in team fights.
-Bargain Hunter is the better option for scaling. Tenacity for sustainability is also a solid choice, but grasping late game properly and complete is a hurdle without reducing the item costs.
-Killing Spree further enhances here capability to chase enemies. Aside from the first proposition, this talent let her outplay enemies in a pinch to pinch situation. The instant recovery is annexed to her incredible recovery kit, letting her rampage the field without fearing damages.

equips.equip_desc:
-CC Reducing Items:
Tough Boots and Brute Force Breastplate is the CC Reduction tandem. These items let Freya move more often in a crowd controlled spaces.
-Attack Speed Items:
Corrosion Scythe, Golden Staff, and Sea Halberd are Freya's core item. These item choices are a balance of attack speed and attack. CS slows rather agile enemies, letting her compromise their charges. GS provide bonus for recovering her stack after each 3 hits, allowing her to overlap passive stack. SH provide countermeasure for opposing healers.
-CD Reduction Items:
Brute Force Breastplate and Oracle will allow Freya to spam here Ultimate more often. Aside from the BFB's CC reducing aid, this item can also help Freya gain attack. Oracle help Freya get leniency upon the anti-heal penalty.

-Some might object, "Why not use Demon Hunter Sword(DHS) instead of Sea Halberd?". Well, there are 2 reasons: 
1) DHS is inferior compared to CS and GS, as both of these have higher caliber of attack speed bonus. 
2) Freya's ultimate relies on attack not attack speed for bonus in terms of recovered HP, which DHS fails to provide so. In contrast to SH which also provides anti-recovery - good for opposing fighters.

hero.hero_strength:
-Freya is a late game dominator. Her arsenals are meant to chase opponents tenaciously, all whilst taking considerable amount of damage.
-Freya is a solid choice for countering slow yet squishy heroes that reluctantly give up damage for mobility. Layla, Hanabi, and Melissa are one of the fewer inclusions.
hero.hero_weakness: -Freya finds herself in trouble with scaling, An improper build priority compensates the fact that she is reliant to it. Aside from this critical predicament, Freya is tanky yet fragile, akin to Alucard's kit. Both of them struggle in sustaining damage inside of crowd controls, where their capability to recover are denied upon.
